sourceMovie.title: Scoop
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Scoop (2006) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Woody Allen's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Murder Mystery
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Scoop clicked for you, Murder Mystery (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and mystery, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Scoop?
faq.answer: Try Murder Mystery, Fletch, Family Plot—each shares DNA with Scoop while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Scoop?
faq.answer: Scoop (2006) is best described as Comedy, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Scoop worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
